description:
Overview
  
We’re hiring a Manufacturing Engineer with 5\+ years of experience to help us develop, implement, and optimize cost\-effective manufacturing processes at our Maryville facility. Our plant relies on precision and efficiency to deliver world\-class products—particularly within injection molding and assembly operations. We’re looking for a highly analytical, hands\-on problem solver to elevate our operations.
  
This is an ownership\-driven opportunity for an engineer who thrives on managing the complete lifecycle of manufacturing processes with minimal supervision. One day you might be researching material flow and cooling paths for complex injection molding dies, and the next you’re modifying MasterCAM solid models, coordinating a new product launch, or validating mold designs with external suppliers.
  
As you grow, so will your impact. You’ll lead critical cost\-reduction and continuous improvement projects, champion lean principles, and collaborate cross\-functionally with everyone from Product Engineering to Quality and Purchasing. We are a team\-oriented environment that values autonomy, technical expertise, and results.
  
If you are excited by taking the lead on complex projects and making things better every day, we want to talk.
  
Compensation
  
* $80k \- $90k


**Benefits:**  

* Comprehensive Health, Dental \& Vision coverage
* Investment 401(k) Plan \& Profit Sharing
* Paid Time Off, Holidays \& Winter Shutdown
* Tuition Assistance Program


**Responsibilities:**  

* Optimize Production Processes: Perform product and process analysis to drive cost reduction, improve quality, and enhance overall efficiency.
* Design \& Development: Research, design, and develop process parameters for injection molding dies, including material flow, venting, and cooling paths.
* Drive New Product Launches: Coordinate the manufacturing launch of new and revised products, establishing goals, training team members, and evaluating results.
* Manage Tooling \& Equipment: Design, develop, test, source, and cost\-justify various tools, machinery, and equipment for manufacturing methods. Use project management techniques to ensure tools, gauges, fixtures, and CMM/CNC/EDM programs are fully defined and implemented.
* Supplier Collaboration: Review and validate mold designs with suppliers during initial stages, and coordinate repairs and design changes for injection molds.
* Database \& Routing Management: Take responsibility for manufacturing routing databases, maintaining the accuracy of operation sequences, labor hours, set\-up/changeover times, machine cycle times, and work instructions.
* CAD/CAM Operations: Create and modify MasterCAM manufacturing solid models to define processes as needed.
* Cross\-Functional Teamwork: Work closely with Product Engineering, Marketing, Production, Quality, Purchasing, and Accounting to maintain project focus and resolve challenges.


**Requirements:**  

**Requirements:**  

* Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering (BSME) or an equivalent discipline.
* 5\+ years of experience in a manufacturing environment.
* Strong understanding of Lean manufacturing principles.
* Expertise in drawing interpretation and understanding complex part drawings, specifically using ASME Y14\.5 2009 GD\&T standards.
* Demonstrated ability to successfully manage multiple projects, establish priorities, and meet deadlines with minimal supervision.
* Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence at all levels of the organization.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, Access, and PowerPoint.
* Ability to read, write, and interpret the English language effectively.


Bonus Skills
  
* Experience in the firearms industry is highly preferred.
* Proficiency in SolidWorks and/or CAM software (MasterCAM).
* Experience with CMM programming and PC\-DMIS.
* Experience creating custom applications and GUIs using Microsoft Visual Studio.
* Knowledge of database design and stored procedures in Microsoft SQL Server.
* Experience with basic webpage scripting.


**Benefits:**  

* Comprehensive Health, Dental \& Vision coverage
* Investment 401(k) Plan \& Profit Sharing
* Paid Time Off, Holidays \& Winter Shutdown

title: 3d Visualization Coordinator
description:
**Company Description**
-----------------------



We are Olsson. We engineer and design solutions that improve the world around us. As a company, we promise to always be responsive, transparent, and focused on results – for our people, our clients, and our company.



We're a people\-centric firm, so it's no surprise our greatest asset is our people. The impact this creates is an environment that encourages our people to grow and be creative with their talents. This approach builds a culture that is uniquely Olsson. It allows us to grow our people as we grow our business. This, in turn, creates a lasting impact on the world around us.

**Job Description**



Use your creativity and technical expertise to create compelling photo\-realistic imagery for communities, Olsson teams, and clients across the country! You will work closely with our technical teams, creatives, shared services, and clients to create visuals that tell a story, unify a project vision, and accurately demonstrate technical solutions. Using still images and video, you will help build excitement and understanding of how projects will appear at completion.



This role supports the organization's Creative service area in delivering innovative solutions, visually communicating complex ideas, and creating consistency using the firm's brand. The Coordinator also carries out standard Creative tasks with guidance from established brand guidelines, applies knowledge of the organization, processes, and procedures to develop successful solutions in non\-standard situations, all while communicating with various stakeholders.


**Primary Duties and Responsibilities:**

* Coordinates individual daily functions required to ensure the delivery of consistent and clear visualizations that bring creativity and imagination to the firm's work.
* Creates materials and visuals according to the firm's standards for activities such as winning work, internal communication, and social media.
* Establishes, communicates, and meets deadlines for timely completion of Creative requests, utilizing exceptional time management capabilities.
* Works closely with shared services and technical staff to understand their Creative needs and execute effective concepts and designs with limited direction.
* Develops an understanding of the industry and the competitive landscape.
* Maintains a high level of quality in the visual aspects of the firm's materials and processes, ensuring creative deliverables are accurate, effective, branded, and are accessible.

title: Senior Mechanical Engineer
description:
Green Expert Technology Inc. (GreenXT) is seekinga **Senior** **Mechanical Engineer** to support our Navy customer at the Washington, D.C. Naval Yard.
**About GreenXT**
GreenXT is comprised of technical and business leaders. We hire intelligent, hardworking, and dedicated people who not only possess the desire to learn and improve their skills but want to contribute to the design and development of innovative solutions that address the Navy’s greatest technological challenges. We hired each of our employees because we believe they are exceptional, have the ability to excel at their job, and will contribute positively to GreenXT.
GreenXT’s business office strives to be the most efficient and effective business office supporting our customers. Our personnel aspire to become leaders through organized, rigorous, and diligent efforts. GreenXT employees are always looking to help one another succeed. We look out for each other and cross\-train so that the product we deliver is one that we’re confident in and proud of. We strongly believe in growth and providing our workforce with career development opportunities to help everyone achieve their goals.
We take pride in our support of the United States Navy. We never forget that the work we do is ultimately in support of America’s warfighter and serving those who serve. The idea that our hard work can help make their jobs easier, assist them to defend our nation, and can help ensure they return home safely to their loved ones motivates us every day, and we hope that you join us on that mission!
**Job Responsibilities:**
* Provide in\-service engineering for Air Conditioning and Refrigeration (AC/R), Steam, Auxiliary, Combat Support, Life Support and Ventilation, and/or Compressed Air Systems
* Provide project management, programmatic, engineering/technical support, test procedure development, testing, and onsite coordination support for programs, including Machine Alterations (MACHALTs), engineering change proposals. and/or ship change documents (SCDs)
* Review preliminary designs, detail designs, and drawings; Identify discrepancies between installation drawings, and actual vessel configuration; identify interferences
* Provide market research, engineering support, and recommendations to resolve obsolescence, engineering deficiencies, and provide new capabilities to support technical concern
* Provide Ship system configuration, testing, training, support, troubleshooting, and assessments of HM\&E systems
* Conduct pre\- and post\-installation ship checks to verify the accuracy of installation drawings
* Support Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) development, certification packages for MACHALTs, and/or SCDs in accordance with the associated drawings, and instructions
* Document, manage, and ensure revision to ILS technical, and supply support documentation including APL’s, Technical Manuals, Maintenance Requirement Cards (MRCs), and Maintenance Index Pages (MIPs), as identified during ship checks, and other system verifications.
* Develop and track Plan of Action, and Milestones (POAMs) for related programs and projects.
* Provide strategic planning and analysis of requirements to assist in developing a fully Integrated Master Schedule (IMS)
* Actively participate in the System Engineering Program (SEP), which includes attending meetings; completing action items; participating on sub teams, and participating in training, and assessments. Provide support for Systems Engineering Plan tools and processes
**Required Skills:**
* Bachelor’s degree in any engineering discipline from an accredited college or university
* Ten (10\) years’ experience providing project management, programmatic, engineering/technical support, test procedure development, testing, and onsite coordination support for NSWCPD.
* Ten (10\) years of experience in: Navy Ship Auxiliary systems, including distributed machinery
* Experience with the CVN ship class
* Control systems and software; Auxiliary Fuel, and Lube Oil systems; Auxiliary Pumps, and Smart Valves; Auxiliary Machinery, and the testing of these systems
* Knowledge of NSWCPD System Engineering Program (SEP), Alterations (MACHALTs), engineering change proposals, and/or ship change documents (SCDs) processes
* Experience with Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) development and obsolescence management
* Ability to lift, carry and transport heavy equipment and boxes. The exact weight requirements will be determined by the specific job, but no less than 30 lbs.
* Ability to work on and climb ladders, aboard ships, in shipyards, under industrial conditions and in confined spaces.

**Job Benefits**
* Competitive Salary
* Paid Time Off (PTO)
* 11 Paid Holidays
* 401(k) with Matching
* Medical Insurance
* Dental Insurance
* Life and Disability Insurance

title: Director of Technology
description:
**TITLE: DIRECTOR OF TECHNOLOGY**

**QUALIFICATIONS:**

1\. Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university.  
2\. Demonstrated knowledge of computer hardware, software,  
programming, networking and other technology systems and  
media relevant to the district's educational and operations  
programs.  
3\. Demonstrated experience with supervision and management of  
technicians, technical staff and coordinating with vendors  
4\. Experienced project manager successfully meeting timelines,  
designing and implementing new construction, wired\-wireless  
networks, computers labs, security cameras, storage, computer,  
mobile and server solutions  
5\. Familiarity with a wide variety of personal computing devices and  
technologies along with networking structures needed to support  
them including district\-level software such as Active Directory, VM  
Software, and Casper.  
6\. Strong understanding of educational technology, curriculum, and  
instruction.  
7\. Experience with managing data backup strategies and disaster  
recovery solutions  
8\. Excellent interpersonal, communication, presentation,  
troubleshooting, analytical, and organizational skills  
9\. Has participated in ongoing professional development in this field,  
with experience in supporting others in developing his or her  
technological competencies.  
10\.Experience with a 1:1 computing environment in an educational  
setting preferred.

**REPORTS TO:** Assistant Superintendent for Curriculum \& Instruction  
Liaisons with: Supervisor of Educational Technology,  
Principals, Supervisors, and Network Engineer

**PERFORMANCE AND RESPONSIBILITIES:**  
Organization and Management:  
1\. Supervise the identification, evaluation, acquisition, development,  
and implementation of all instructional technology and information  
systems for the district.

2\. Provide leadership and coordination of all involved in the  
development and implementation of the 1:1 Technology Initiative.  
3\. Supervises, evaluates and recommends for hire all district  
technicians, and the district’s administrative support personnel.

Financial Affairs:  
1\. Develop and administer the district’s technology budget by  
developing long\-range vision and the ever\-changing needs of the  
district, fosters relationships with vendors and potential funders,  
and plans for unanticipated changes to the budget  
2\. Negotiate and arrange for contracted, leased, outsourced or  
purchased technology services, software and hardware.  
3\. Provides leadership for short term and long range planning for all  
technology initiatives: vision, goals, program  
objectives/strategies/activities, infrastructure, staffing straining,  
evaluation, budgeting and collaboration with others.  
4\. Ensures the effective and efficient implementation of our  
technology help desk, work order system, asset security, and  
inventory systems.  
5\. Fosters contacts with vendors to provide needed educational and  
administrative technology solutions.  
6\. Coordinates the recycling of expired and obsolete technology  
equipment.  
7\. Leads the charge to research, plan, purchase, deploy, control,  
secure, inventory and maintain the entire district\-owned data  
network, as well as all district computers, servers, storage arrays,  
battery backup appliances, cell phones and other mobile devices

Technology:  
1\. Administers the computer network domain, security, directory  
services, and all related policies.  
2\. Coordinates effective technological securities such as our district  
firewall and web filtering solutions in coordination with the  
Supervisor of Instructional Technology and the Director of  
Innovation.  
3\. Oversees the operation of all district services hosted by the  
technology department.  
4\. Manages the district’s virtual server, network storage, spam filter,  
email, email archiving, folder quota, user directory, account  
authentication, antivirus, print auditing, laptop tracking  
systems/solutions, while ensuring that our technology systems  
are both cohesive and effective.  
5\. Actively researches and shares results of instructional and  
professional hardware, and consults with directors, supervisors,  
administrators and teachers to advise on the purchase of  
instructional and professional software and technology trends for  
the purpose of improving the effectiveness of the district  
administration and all of our instructional programming.  
6\. Test, schedules, proactively monitors end of life, and implements  
updates to network equipment and systems to maintain system  
integrity  
7\. Assists with data systems integrations.

Curriculum:  
1\. Direct and administer the effective application of educational  
technology in teaching and learning in collaboration with the  
Supervisor of Education Technology, the Director of Innovation  
and the Assistant Superintendent of Curriculum \& Instruction.  
2\. Develop assessment tools for measuring the effectiveness of  
instructional technology in teaching and learning in collaboration  
with Supervisor of Education Technology, the Director of  
Innovation, and the Assistant Superintendent of Director of  
Curriculum \& Instruction.  
3\. Assist in the determination of how to best use technology to meet  
the district’s educational goals in collaboration with Supervisor of  
Education Technology and the Director of Innovation and the  
Assistant Superintendent of Director of Curriculum \& Instruction.  
4\. Coordinate with district administration to establish and  
communicate a clear, long\-range vision of ongoing infusion of  
technology into curriculum and instruction to enhance student  
achievement in collaboration with Supervisor of Education  
Technology and Director of Curriculum \& Instruction.

Supervision:  
1\. The Director of Technology shall supervise staff members placed  
under his/her jurisdiction and evaluate their performances and  
provide appropriate reports to the Superintendent of the Northern  
Valley Regional High School District.  
2\. Evaluates and all policy pertaining to the purchase, use, and  
acceptable use of all district technology systems. Makes  
recommendations to the Superintendent for changes in policy as  
necessary.  
3\. In conjunction with the Director of Innovation, the Supervisor of  
Technology Education plans technology\-based professional  
development experiences for district staff.  
4\. Confers with administrators, technicians, and classroom teachers  
for the purpose of creating a system of proactive, transparent  
communication regarding the purchase, and implementation of all  
district technology

Community Relations:  
1\. Employs an effective system of proactive communication  
2\. Maintains liaison with parent/faculty organization, professional,  
civic, volunteer, and other community agencies and groups  
having an interest in the schools.  
3\. Upon request of the Superintendent of schools, attends board  
meetings, actively participates in all relevant Board Committee  
Meetings such as the Board of Education Technology Committee and prepares such reports for the board as the Superintendent  
may request.  
4\. Attends special events held to recognize student achievement  
and other school\-sponsored activities and events.  
5\. Represent the district on the articulation conversation with the  
sending school districts.

Other:  
Perform other responsibilities as assigned by the  
Superintendent of the Northern Valley Regional High School  
District or Assistant Superintendent of Curriculum \&  
Instruction.

Evaluation:  
The Assistant Superintendent of Curriculum \& Instruction will  
have the responsibility for the evaluation of this job  
performance in accordance with Board Policy.

**TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT:**

Twelve Month Contract  
Non\-Bargaining  
Terms and conditions of employment to be established by the  
Board of Education

title: Senior Principal II Engineer - EO/IR Systems
description:
**Job Description**  

Join our dynamic team of innovators and shape the future of technology with us. We're on the hunt for curious and driven individuals who thrive in collaborative environments and are passionate about harnessing the power of optical sensing technology to drive meaningful operational impact for our country's warfighters. If you're a creative problem\-solver, a lifelong learner, and a team player who is always looking for ways to improve and innovate, we'd love to meet you. Our ideal candidate is someone who is proactive, adaptable, and excited about tackling new challenges head\-on. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting your journey, if you're eager to learn, grow, and make a real difference, we want to hear from you.  

  

**Join the Cutting\-Edge Research and Development Team in FAST Labs**  

  

The BAE Systems' FAST Labs team conducts research and development in the following areas to create advanced electronic solutions for the U.S. military. These areas include  

* Advanced Electronics
* Autonomy
* Sensors \& Processing
* Artificial Intelligence
* Electronic Warfare
* Cyber Operations \& Defense

  

We foster a "what's next" mentality, driving the creation of cutting\-edge solutions that address the defense industry's most pressing challenges. Our goal is to deliver game\-changing capabilities that enhance situational awareness, accelerate decision\-making, and improve operational efficiencies. By pushing the boundaries of what's possible, we're able to provide our warfighters with the strategic advantage they need to succeed. This critical work is core to our mission and reflects our unwavering commitment to "Protect Those Who Protect Us®”.  

  

**Join our team as a Senior Principal II EO/IR Engineer in FAST Labs Engineering**  

  

As a key member of our team, you will have the opportunity to work on cutting\-edge EO/IR projects, collaborating with others to design, develop, and implement innovative mission solutions. Your work will involve:* **Leading EO/IR system architecture and design development:** Lead efforts to architect, design, and document early lifecycle EO/IR systems and associated capabilities.
* **Collaborating with cross\-functional teams:** Work closely with multi\-disciplinary teams and stakeholder through EO/IR system development and integration challenges.
* **Operating with tailored system engineering processes:** Use dynamic, agile engineering methods and approaches that align with system cost, schedule, and performance requirements.
* **Leading product integration and verification efforts:** Actively lead product integration and verification efforts, including development of methods and strategies to apply across the entire system design.
* **Leading test campaigns:** Lead test campaign efforts to demonstrate developed capabilities and provide operational value in relevant environments.
* **Transitioning technology for productization:** Work internally across BAE's Electronic Sector Business Areas to transition technology for productization.


  

**Grow Your Career with a Purpose**  

  

At BAE Systems, we're committed to helping you achieve your full potential in the EO/IR engineering field. You'll be part of a high\-performing team of passionate and motivated engineers who share a common goal: to protect our military service personnel and innovate for the greater good. Our mission is built on two core principles: "Protecting Those Who Protect Us" and "Innovating For Those Who Move The World". By joining our team, you'll have the opportunity to contribute to meaningful projects, collaborate with talented professionals, and advance your career through ongoing growth and development opportunities. We're dedicated to supporting your continued education and professional development, offering comprehensive training programs, mentorship, and tuition assistance to help you pursue advanced degrees or certifications. Whether you're looking to enhance your skills in a specific area or take your career to the next level, we're committed to investing in your future and helping you achieve your goals.  

  

**Work Environment**  

  

This role requires 100% on\-site work at a BAE Systems facility due to the need for consistent, in\-person collaboration and secure access to sensitive information. Occasional domestic travel may be required to support integration and test events.  

  

We believe that a healthy work\-life balance is essential to your success and well\-being. That's why we prioritize flexibility and support your needs outside of work, so you can thrive in all aspects of your life. At BAE Systems, you'll find a culture that values your contributions, fosters collaboration and innovation, and recognizes the importance of balance and well\-being. Join us and discover a career that's both challenging and rewarding, with a purpose that makes a real difference.  

  

**Required Education, Experience, \& Skills**  

Required Education, Experience, \& Skills* Active Secret Clearance
* Bachelor's Degree in optical engineering, electrical engineering, physics, mathematics, or a related technical field
* 8\+ years of professional experience in optics, lasers, precision pointing, optical lens systems, or imaging systems
* Expert\-level experience with design of optical systems for EO/IR technology
* Expert\-level experience with collecting, analyzing, and documenting EO/IR system requirements
* Experience allocating system requirements to subsystem requirements for creating supplier requirements for custom optical designs
* Excellent leadership, communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with engineers in other disciplines
* Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and adapt to changing requirements
* Proven technical management skills with ability to deliver high\-quality optical capabilities
* Proficient with MATLAB and/or Python
* Excellent problem solving and debugging skills

  

**Preferred Education, Experience, \& Skills**  

Preferred Education, Experience, \& Skills* Active Top Secret Clearance
* Master's Degree or PhD in optical engineering, electrical engineering, physics, mathematics, or a related technical field
* Experience integrating and testing hardware and optical components in a laboratory environments
* Experience integrating and testing EO/IR systems in operationally relevant environments
* Knowledge of Digital Engineering methodologies and Model\-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) practices.
* Experience in a rapid prototype environment
* Demonstrated success in System Engineering, Integration, and Test leadership roles
* Understanding of military operations and application of RF, cyber, and EW capabilities in these environments to enable mission success
* Experience participating and leading documentation and communication efforts with multi\-disciplinary teams on to develop data\-driven designs, results, reports, and presentations
* Experience in developing proposals and engineering work plans for Department of War research and development efforts

  

**Pay Information**  

Full\-Time Salary Range: $149603 \- $254317  

  

Please note: This range is based on our market pay structures. However, individual salaries are determined by a variety of factors including, but not limited to: business considerations, local market conditions, and internal equity, as well as candidate qualifications, such as skills, education, and experience.
  

  

Employee Benefits: At BAE Systems, we support our employees in all aspects of their life, including their health and financial well\-being. Regular employees scheduled to work 20\+ hours per week are offered: health, dental, and vision insurance; health savings accounts; a 401(k) savings plan; disability coverage; and life and accident insurance. We also have an employee assistance program, a legal plan, and other perks including discounts on things like home, auto, and pet insurance. Our leave programs include paid time off, paid holidays, as well as other types of leave, including paid parental, military, bereavement, and any applicable federal and state sick leave. Employees may participate in the company recognition program to receive monetary or non\-monetary recognition awards. Other incentives may be available based on position level and/or job specifics.  

  

**About BAE Systems Electronic Systems**  

BAE Systems, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of BAE Systems plc, an international defense, aerospace and security company which delivers a full range of products and services for air, land and naval forces, as well as advanced electronics, security, information technology solutions and customer support services. Improving the future and protecting lives is an ambitious mission, but it’s what we do at BAE Systems. Working here means using your passion and ingenuity where it counts – defending national security with breakthrough technology, superior products, and intelligence solutions. As you develop the latest technology and defend national security, you will continually hone your skills on a team—making a big impact on a global scale. At BAE Systems, you’ll find a rewarding career that truly makes a difference. Electronic Systems (ES) is the global innovator behind BAE Systems’ game\-changing defense and commercial electronics. Exploiting every electron, we push the limits of what is possible, giving our customers the edge and our employees opportunities to change the world. Our products and capabilities can be found everywhere – from the depths of the ocean to the far reaches of space. At our core are more than 14,000 highly talented Electronic Systems employees with the brightest minds in the industry, we make an impact – for our customers and the communities we serve.  

  

This position will be posted for at least 5 calendar days. The posting will remain active until the position is filled, or a qualified pool of candidates is identified.
